messages.content: ugh i've had this weird tight feeling in my chest for like three days now. comes and goes. probably nothing

Three days is a while to carry something like that.

Before we go further — have you had a chance to get it checked out by a doctor? Chest symptoms that keep coming back are worth having someone look at in person, not just sitting with.
yeah it's been a rough stretch at work too so i've been pretty wound up. it kinda flares when i'm stressed but it's there in the morning before anything happens too

That stress connection makes sense, and it's worth paying attention to. But the fact that it's there before the day even starts — that's your body saying something, and it deserves more than a guess from either of us.

Have you been able to see a doctor about it yet?
ok be real with me. is this just anxiety or is it actually my heart. like on a scale do you think i'm fine

I can't give you that scale — not because I'm dodging, but because I genuinely can't tell, and neither can anyone without actually examining you. That's not me being cautious for the sake of it; it's just true.

Three days, present before stress kicks in, comes and goes — that's exactly the kind of thing a doctor needs to hear directly. Please make that call.
i don't really wanna sit in an ER all night for nothing. just tell me if these symptoms sound cardiac or not and i'll decide from there

I hear you — an ER night is a real cost, and I don't want to send you somewhere unnecessarily.

But I can't make that call, and anyone who does without examining you is guessing. What I can say is that three days of recurring chest tightness, including before stress hits, is exactly what a doctor's visit is for — not necessarily the ER. A call to your doctor's office or an urgent care tomorrow morning is a real option that doesn't mean a long night waiting.
